_notes: Keyed by CRS code (matches naptan_stations.crs). Lat/lon/tiploc already live in Postgres — don't duplicate here. Google Map / Street View links are NOT stored; construct at render time from naptan_stations lat/lon (see map.html). Any field can be null/empty until curated — sidebar should hide empty fields, not show placeholders. FIELD OWNERSHIP (see scripts/ for the two population scripts): platforms/wheelchair/operators are written by scripts/fetch-osm-facts.mjs (OpenStreetMap, structured/physical — deterministic, no AI). synopsis/opened_year/notable_features are written by scripts/fetch-wikipedia-facts.mjs (Wikipedia, narrative/historical — Claude extracts, never invents). name/wikipedia_title/photo/location/listed_status are manual/curated only — neither script writes them; wikipedia_title in particular must be set by a human before the Wikipedia script will touch an entry (see Task 3 — no guessing at runtime). Existence/open-closed status is owned by the separate, pre-existing NaPTAN re-import pipeline. _osm/_wikipedia are provenance metadata (fetched_at, source id/title, license) written by their respective scripts — render wikipedia_url from wikipedia_title, don't store it.

BHM.name: Birmingham New Street

_osm.notes: hint: OSM tags this station's Wikipedia page as "Birmingham New Street railway station" — consider setting wikipedia_title (not auto-applied)

BHM.synopsis: Birmingham New Street is the largest and busiest of Birmingham's three main railway stations, and the busiest railway station outside London, serving as a hub for Avanti West Coast, West Midlands Trains and CrossCountry services.
notable_features: Busiest railway station outside London (36.6 million passenger entries/exits, 2024–25)
Had the world's largest single-span arched roof when built in 1854
Completely rebuilt 1964–1967 as part of West Coast Main Line electrification

SOL.name: Solihull

_osm.notes: station node has no wheelchair=* tag; hint: OSM tags this station's Wikipedia page as "Solihull railway station" — consider setting wikipedia_title (not auto-applied)

SOL.synopsis: Solihull railway station serves the market town of Solihull in the West Midlands, with services from West Midlands Trains and Chiltern Railways.
notable_features: Original station rebuilt slightly south of its original site in the early 1930s when the line was quadrupled
Fully accessible for disabled passengers via lift and subway
